latest.title: Appointment of Chief Revenue Officer
latest.content:
26 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1669Y
dotDigital Group plc
26 March 2026
26 March 2026
Dotdigital Group plc
("Dotdigital" or the "Group")
Appointment of Chief Revenue Officer
Dotdigital Group (AIM: DOTD), the leading SaaS provider of an AI-powered customer experience and data platform for intelligent personalised marketing engagement at scale, is pleased to announce the appointment of Mike Harris as Chief Revenue Officer (non-board position), effective 1 April 2026.
The Chief Revenue Officer role is a newly created Group position, established to bring all global go
‑
to
‑
market functions together under unified leadership as the Group continues to scale internationally. The role reports directly to the Chief Executive Officer and is responsible for leading Dotdigital's global commercial organisation and revenue performance, with a focus on driving more consistent execution across regions and improving expansion, retention and customer lifetime value.
Mike has served as Dotdigital's Interim GM for EMEA since July 2025, during which time he has made a strong contribution to the Group's commercial strategy and revenue performance in the region.
Mike brings extensive senior leadership experience in scaling revenue across global B2B SaaS and marketing technology businesses. Most recently, he served as CRO at Haiilo, where he drove several growth, retention and efficiency-related initiatives. Prior to this, he held senior commercial leadership roles including Vice President of Global Sales at parcelLab and Senior Vice-President of Global Sales at Monetate, both marketing technology platforms. Mike has also held senior roles at Bluecore and Omniture.
Milan Patel, CEO of Dotdigital, commented:
"Dotdigital has strengthened significantly in recent years through sustained product investment and targeted acquisitions, building a broader, more capable platform. We will continue to invest as we extend that advantage, while at the same time focusing on raising the consistency and pace of execution globally. Creating the Chief Revenue Officer role gives us clear leadership of the Group's commercial engine and reinforces our ambition to scale internationally with discipline.
"Since joining us as interim GM for EMEA last summer, Mike has raised the bar on focus, pace and commercial discipline. I am delighted he will now lead this agenda as Chief Revenue Officer as we deepen customer success, drive expansion and retention, and deliver sustainable, high-quality recurring revenue growth."
Mike Harris, incoming CRO, commented:
"Over the past several months I have seen a platform with real strength, a loyal customer base, and a team that cares deeply about what we do - and we know that our success is ultimately defined by the success of our customers. As CRO, my priority is to help our teams focus on the right opportunities, make performance visible through clear metrics, and support customers to realise value faster. I am excited to take on the role and help drive the next stage of growth."
Contacts
Dotdigital Group plc
+44 (0)20 3953 3072
Milan Patel, CEO
investorrelations@dotdigital.com
Tom Mullan, CFO
Alma Strategic Communications
+44 (0)20 3405 0210
Hilary Buchanan
dotdigital@almastrategic.com
David Ison
Will Merison
Canaccord Genuity (Nominated Adviser and Joint Broker)
+44 (0)20 7523 8000
Bobbie Hilliam
Elizabeth Halley-Stott
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited (Joint Broker)
+44 (0)20 7220 0500
Jonny Franklin-Adams, Corporate Finance
Sunila de Silva, Equity Capital Markets
Singer Capital Markets (Joint Broker)
+44 (0)20 7496 3000
Shaun Dobson
Jen Boorer
About Dotdigital
Dotdigital Group plc (AIM: DOTD) is a leading provider of cross-channel marketing automation technology to marketing professionals. Dotdigital's customer experience and data platform (CXDP) combines the power of automation and AI to help businesses deliver hyper-relevant customer experiences at scale. With Dotdigital, marketing teams can unify and enrich their customer data, identify valuable customer segments, and deliver personalised cross-channel customer journeys that result in engagements, conversions, and loyalty.
Founded in 1999, Dotdigital is headquartered in London with offices in Manchester, Southampton, New York, Melbourne, Sydney, Singapore, Tokyo, Warsaw and Cape Town. Dotdigital's solutions empower over 4,000 brands across 150 countries.

company_30s.what_they_do: Dotdigital (DOTD.L) builds and sells a cloud‑based marketing automation platform. It lets businesses design, send and measure email, SMS, push and social campaigns, and it stitches customer data into one view so marketers can target the right people at the right time.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global spend on digital marketing is now over $300 billion and growing faster than traditional media. Companies need tools that can automate, personalize and analyse campaigns at scale. Dotdigital’s all‑in‑one platform competes with big names like Salesforce Marketing Cloud and HubSpot, but it focuses on mid‑market brands that want a simpler, cheaper solution with deep analytics.
company_30s.current_state: Dotdigital is in a growth phase. It has recently bought a smaller agency (Alia) to add content services, hired a new Chief Revenue Officer and is actively buying back shares. The company is still in a cash‑burn mode, but the moves suggest it is trying to accelerate revenue and broaden its product suite.

rns_narrative: Dotdigital is reshaping its leadership and expanding its product line: the appointment of a new CRO and the acquisition of Alia signal a push to boost sales and content capabilities.
Shareholder activity shows the board is tightening control – directors are increasing their stakes and the company is buying back shares, a sign of confidence in the business.

Financial reporting is in the works: interim results, a notice of results and a half‑year update are coming. These filings will reveal whether the new hires and acquisition are translating into revenue growth.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Skeptical silence with a few hopeful whispers
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders see the acquisition and CRO as a catalyst for a rebound, citing the platform’s strong niche in mid‑market e‑commerce.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others worry that AI‑driven competitors will erode Dotdigital’s market share and that the recent share buy‑back could be a sign of desperation.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has stayed flat; the last few posts are a mix of bullish speculation and bearish caution, but overall engagement is minimal.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no one is talking about Dotdigital, which is a good sign for a contrarian play.

near_term_catalysts: The integration of Alia’s content team could unlock new revenue streams and provide fresh case studies for sales.
The new CRO’s first quarterly update is due in Q2 – a clear sign‑post for whether the sales push is working.
Any announcement of a new product feature or partnership (e.g., a deeper AI‑driven recommendation engine) would be a strong bounce trigger.
A positive earnings surprise or a higher-than‑expected revenue growth rate would validate the expansion strategy.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in trading volume, a breakout above the recent resistance level, or a clear statement from the CRO about a new revenue target. Also keep an eye on the share price approaching the 50‑day moving average – that’s a common support level for mean‑reversion plays.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a deep crash (70‑95 % down). The APEX compression score of 15/21 indicates the price is in a tight range, suggesting a potential breakout is imminent. The next few weeks will be critical: if the price holds above the low of the current range, a reversal is likely.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: A 70 % plunge means the price is at the bottom of a long‑term decline. If it rebounds to halfway between the trough and the pre‑crash level, that’s roughly a 35 % gain from the bottom. The recent acquisition and new CRO raise the probability that the company will start generating higher revenue, which is the main driver for the mean‑reversion thesis.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the acquisition fails to deliver new revenue, or if AI competitors capture the mid‑market niche, the price could slide further. The worst case is a 10‑15 % additional drop before a recovery, which would still leave the stock below its pre‑crash valuation.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 35 % from the trough. Potential downside: capped at about 15 % if the price falls further before a bounce. The asymmetry is clearly in favour of a contrarian long.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the high of the current compression range. Use 10 % of your discretionary capital, set a stop 20 % below the entry to protect against a false breakout. Take profits in stages: 50 % at the halfway point, 50 % at the pre‑crash level.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume surge of at least 30 % above the 20‑day average combined with a positive earnings release or a clear revenue target from the CRO. That will be the green light to enter.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks have surfaced yet. If a bankruptcy filing or a regulatory delisting notice appears, abandon the position immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Dotdigital’s 70 % plunge, coupled with a fresh acquisition and new sales leadership, creates a high‑asymmetry contrarian opportunity that could double the price from the trough within the next few months.
